title: fix(media): harden upload endpoint (file size, magic bytes, logging)
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: The undo button arrives
gist.blurb: We've hardened the media upload pipes and added a shiny new DELETE endpoint. Now you can scrub your uploads if you change your mind.
gist.summary: Added a secure DELETE endpoint for media uploads and simplified validation logic to treat files as binary data.
gist.impact: Developers can now programmatically remove their uploaded media, and the upload system handles binary data more reliably.
gist.visual_concept: The 'delete' function is best represented by the Nomnom creature consuming data. The scene should show the media service as a pipeline or conveyor belt where unwanted files are fed to Nomnom.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round nomnom creature happily eating a pixelated photo frame labeled 'DELETE'. The bee mascot hovers nearby holding a security clipboard. Lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic. Text in pixel font: 'DELETED'. Nostalgic but beautiful.
